Package com.wip.controller

Class CustomerUiController

java.lang.Object
com.wip.controller.CustomerUiController
@Controller @RequestMapping("/customers") public class CustomerUiController extends Object
Thymeleaf UI controller for managing customer records in the Courier Tracking System.

Handles all browser-facing HTTP requests under the /customers path and renders server-side HTML views using the Thymeleaf templating engine. This controller covers the complete customer lifecycle — listing, creating, viewing details, editing, and deleting customers — while providing user-friendly error messages and form validation feedback through Spring MVC's BindingResult mechanism.

Page flow overview:

  • GET /customers → customer/list — all customers
  • GET /customers/new → customer/form — blank creation form
  • POST /customers/save → redirect to details or re-render form on error
  • GET /customers/{id} → customer/details — single customer view
  • GET /customers/{id}/edit→ customer/form — pre-filled edit form
  • POST /customers/{id}/update → redirect to details or re-render form on error
  • POST /customers/{id}/delete → redirect to list or render delete error view
Since:
1.0
Version:
1.0
Author:
Dharshan K S (dharshan.ks@wipro.com)

  • Constructor Details

    • CustomerUiController

      public CustomerUiController(CustomerService customerService)
      Constructs a CustomerUiController with the required CustomerService.
      Parameters:
      customerService - the service bean responsible for customer business logic; injected by Spring's dependency injection container

  • Method Details

    • listCustomers

      @GetMapping public String listCustomers(org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Displays the paginated list of all customers.

      Fetches all customer records and populates the model with a customers attribute before rendering the customer/list view template.

      Parameters:
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass attributes to the view; receives the customers attribute (a list of CustomerDto)
      Returns:
      the logical view name "customer/list"

    • showCreateForm

      @GetMapping("/new") public String showCreateForm(org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Displays a blank form for creating a new customer.

      Adds an empty CustomerDto to the model so that the Thymeleaf form can bind to it. The view renders input fields for name, email, phone, and address.

      Parameters:
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass the empty customerDto binding object to the view
      Returns:
      the logical view name "customer/form"

    • saveCustomer

      @PostMapping("/save") public String saveCustomer(@Valid @ModelAttribute("customerDto") @Valid CustomerDto customerDto, org.springframework.validation.BindingResult bindingResult, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Processes the submission of a new customer creation form.

      Validates the submitted CustomerDto via Bean Validation. If validation errors are present, the form is re-rendered with inline error messages. On successful persistence, the user is redirected to the newly created customer's detail page. If the service throws an exception (e.g., duplicate email), an errorMessage attribute is added to the model and the form is re-rendered.

      Parameters:
      customerDto - the form-bound customer data submitted by the user; must satisfy all Bean Validation constraints
      bindingResult - holds validation errors produced during binding and validation
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass error messages back to the view
      Returns:
      a redirect to /customers/{id} on success, or the logical view name "customer/form" when validation or persistence fails

    • customerDetails

      @GetMapping("/{id}") public String customerDetails(@PathVariable Long id, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Displays the detail view for a single customer identified by their ID.

      Looks up the customer by id and adds the result as a customer model attribute. If the customer does not exist, the service layer propagates a ResourceNotFoundException.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier of the customer whose details are to be displayed
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass the customer attribute (a CustomerDto) to the view
      Returns:
      the logical view name "customer/details"
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no customer exists with the given ID

    • editCustomerForm

      @GetMapping("/{id}/edit") public String editCustomerForm(@PathVariable Long id, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Displays a pre-populated form for editing an existing customer's details.

      Loads the current customer data by id and binds it to the customerDto model attribute so that all existing field values are pre-filled in the Thymeleaf form.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier of the customer to be edited
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass the pre-filled customerDto to the view
      Returns:
      the logical view name "customer/form"
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no customer exists with the given ID

    • updateCustomer

      @PostMapping("/{id}/update") public String updateCustomer(@PathVariable Long id, @Valid @ModelAttribute("customerDto") @Valid CustomerDto customerDto, org.springframework.validation.BindingResult bindingResult, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Processes the submission of the customer edit form and persists the updated details.

      Validates the submitted CustomerDto. If validation fails, the form is re-rendered with inline error messages. On success, the user is redirected to the customer's detail page. Handles ResourceNotFoundException when the target customer no longer exists, and generic exceptions for any other unexpected error.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier of the customer being updated
      customerDto - the form-bound updated customer data; must satisfy all Bean Validation constraints
      bindingResult - holds validation errors produced during binding and validation
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass error messages back to the view
      Returns:
      a redirect to /customers/{id} on success, or the logical view name "customer/form" when validation or persistence fails
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no customer exists with the given ID

    • deleteCustomer

      @PostMapping("/{id}/delete") public String deleteCustomer(@PathVariable Long id, org.springframework.ui.Model model, org.springframework.web.servlet.mvc.support.RedirectAttributes redirectAttributes)
      Handles the deletion of a customer and redirects appropriately.

      Attempts to delete the customer identified by id. On success, a flash attribute successMessage is added and the user is redirected to the customer list. If the customer has linked parcels, an IllegalStateException is caught and the customer/delete error view is rendered with an informative message. Any other unexpected exception redirects the user back to the customer list with an error flash message.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier of the customer to delete
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass error messages to the error view
      redirectAttributes - Spring MVC redirect attribute container used to carry flash messages across the redirect boundary
      Returns:
      a redirect to /customers on success or generic error; or the logical view name "customer/delete" when the customer has linked parcels
